senses.definition: a school where students attend classes during the day and return home in the evenings

examples.x: He enrolled his children in the local day school to ensure they could spend evenings together as a family.
The day school offers a wide range of extracurricular activities for students to participate in after classes.
She commutes to the day school from her home in the nearby town, enjoying the balance between academics and family life.
The day school's curriculum focuses on academic excellence while also fostering a sense of community and belonging.
Many parents prefer day schools for their children, appreciating the opportunity for daily interaction and involvement in their education.

senses.definition: a school offering classes in the evening, allowing working adults to attend outside of typical daytime hours

examples.x: He decided to enroll in night school to pursue a degree while working full-time during the day.
The community college offers a variety of vocational courses in its night school program.
She juggled her job during the day and attended night school to further her education and advance her career.
The night school provides flexible scheduling options for students who have other daytime commitments.
Many adults find night school to be a convenient way to acquire new skills or qualifications without disrupting their work schedules.

senses.definition: a school that is funded and operated by the government and is typically open to all students within a certain geographic area

examples.x: She attended the local public school from kindergarten through twelfth grade.
The public school system in this district provides free education to students from diverse backgrounds.
He works as a teacher at the nearest public school.
Public school students often participate in extracurricular activities such as sports and clubs.
The public school board is responsible for making decisions regarding curriculum, policies, and budget allocations.

senses.definition: a school that provides free education due to being funded by the government

examples.x: Every child in our neighborhood attends the local state school, which offers excellent education without any tuition fees.
The government announced additional funding for state schools to improve their facilities and resources.
Unlike private institutions, state schools are accessible to everyone, regardless of their economic background.
My parents decided to enroll me in a state school because of its strong academic reputation and community focus.
She works as a math teacher at a state school, where she loves inspiring students from diverse backgrounds.

senses.definition: a school that receives money from the parents of the students instead of the government

examples.x: She attended a prestigious private school known for its excellent academic record.
The private school offers a wide range of extracurricular activities.
The cost of tuition at a private school can be quite high.
Many parents prefer sending their children to a private school for smaller class sizes.
Private schools often have more resources compared to public institutions.

senses.definition: a private educational institution that operates independently of government control, often funded by tuition fees, donations, and endowments

examples.x: She attends an independent school known for its rigorous academic program and small class sizes.
The prestigious independent school has a long history of academic excellence and strong extracurricular offerings.
He received a scholarship to attend an independent school specializing in arts education.
The local independent school provides a nurturing environment and personalized learning experiences for its students.
The independent school offers a comprehensive curriculum and state-of-the-art facilities to support student learning and development.

senses.definition: a school where students live and study during the school year

examples.x: After much consideration, her parents decided to send her to a prestigious boarding school known for its rigorous academic programs and strong emphasis on character development.
The novel follows a group of friends at a boarding school as they navigate the challenges of adolescence, friendship, and the pressures of academic life.
Boarding school can be a transformative experience for many students, providing them with opportunities for personal growth and independence away from home.
The film depicts the life of a young boy who struggles to fit in at his new boarding school, ultimately discovering his passion for art and making lasting friendships.
Many boarding schools offer a variety of extracurricular activities, from sports to the arts, allowing students to explore their interests and develop new skills outside the classroom.

senses.definition: a public college funded by the government of the state where it is located

examples.x: He decided to enroll at the state university to pursue a degree in engineering.
The state university is known for its affordable tuition rates and diverse student population.
She received a scholarship to attend the honors program at the state university.
The state university's research facilities attract top scholars and scientists from around the world.
He graduated with honors from the state university's business school and went on to pursue a successful career in finance.

senses.definition: a two-year college providing higher education, primarily for students from the local community

examples.x: She decided to attend the community college to save money before transferring to a university.
After graduating from high school, he enrolled in a community college to study business.
Many people choose community college for its affordable tuition compared to four-year universities.
The community college near my house has excellent evening courses for working adults.
He took a few courses at the community college while figuring out what career path he wanted to follow.

senses.definition: a school for students who need extra help with learning because of disabilities or learning difficulties

examples.x: She attends a special school that offers individualized education plans for students with autism spectrum disorder.
The special school provides speech therapy and occupational therapy services to students with communication and motor skills challenges.
He enrolled in a special school that specializes in supporting students with dyslexia and other learning difficulties.
The special school has trained staff and specialized equipment to accommodate students with physical disabilities.
The local education authority provides transportation services for students attending special schools across the district.

senses.definition: (the United States) a public school that is designed to offer specialized programs beyond the standard curriculum; typically located in a large city

examples.x: She applied to a magnet school known for its excellent arts program.
Magnet schools attract students from across the district due to their unique programs.
Parents often prefer magnet schools because of their specialized educational tracks.
The city‚Äôs top magnet school offers specialized courses in science and technology.
He transferred to a magnet school to take advantage of their advanced math curriculum.

senses.definition: an educational institution that provides specialized training and instruction in a particular trade, skill, or profession

examples.x: After high school, she decided to enroll in a vocational school to become a certified electrician.
The vocational school offers programs in automotive technology, culinary arts, and healthcare.
He completed a welding certification program at the local vocational school.
Vocational schools often collaborate with local industries to provide students with hands-on training and job placement opportunities.
Many adults choose to attend vocational schools to acquire new skills or pursue career changes.

senses.definition: a publicly funded school that operates independently from the traditional public school system, often with a specific educational focus or approach

examples.x: The charter school in our neighborhood emphasizes project-based learning and STEM education.
She decided to enroll her child in a charter school because of its smaller class sizes and specialized curriculum.
Charter schools are granted more flexibility in their operations and curriculum compared to traditional public schools.
The local charter school partners with community organizations to offer extracurricular activities and enrichment programs.
Charter schools are accountable to a chartering authority, which may be a local school district, state education agency, or other governing body.

senses.definition: an educational place where people learn practical skills for jobs like engineering or computer technology

examples.x: He enrolled in a polytechnic school to study computer engineering and programming.
The local polytechnic school offers hands-on training in automotive mechanics and repair.
She graduated from a polytechnic school with a degree in electrical engineering.
The polytechnic school collaborates with industry partners to provide internships and job placements for its students.
He chose to attend a polytechnic school to gain practical skills in construction management and project planning.

senses.definition: a university or college where students study subjects related to business, such as economics, management, and finance

examples.x: She decided to apply to business school to pursue a career in management.
Business school can be competitive, but it provides valuable networking opportunities.
After graduating from business school, he landed a job at a major corporation.
She‚Äôs been studying hard for her final exams at business school this semester.
He attended business school to gain the skills needed for a leadership role.

senses.definition: a college or a department in a university where students study medicine to become medical doctors

examples.x: She has always dreamed of attending medical school to become a doctor.
After finishing his undergraduate degree, he plans to apply to medical school.
I heard medical school can be very stressful, with long hours and lots of studying.
She spent four years in medical school before starting her residency at the hospital.
He decided to take a gap year before applying to medical school to gain more experience in healthcare.

senses.definition: an educational institution that provides hands-on training and instruction in specific trades or vocational skills

examples.x: He enrolled in a trade school to learn automotive repair and maintenance.
Trade schools offer programs in various fields, including construction, HVAC, and culinary arts.
Many trade schools have partnerships with local businesses to provide apprenticeships and job placement assistance.
After graduating from trade school, she became a certified electrician and started her own contracting business.
Trade schools typically offer shorter, more focused programs compared to traditional colleges and universities.

senses.definition: a graduate school that offers programs leading to a Juris Doctor degree and prepares students for careers in law

examples.x: Law school typically involves rigorous coursework and practical training in legal research and writing.
After graduating from law school, he passed the bar exam and joined a prestigious law firm.
The law school admissions process is highly competitive, with applicants needing strong academic records and LSAT scores.
Many law schools offer specialized programs in areas such as environmental law, intellectual property, or international law.

senses.definition: a higher education institution that offers academic programs specializing in engineering disciplines

examples.x: He decided to attend an engineering school to pursue a degree in mechanical engineering.
The engineering school at this university is known for its cutting-edge research in aerospace technology.
After graduating from engineering school, she secured a job at a leading technology company.
Engineering schools typically offer a wide range of programs, including civil engineering, electrical engineering, and computer engineering.
Many students choose to attend engineering school to gain the knowledge and skills necessary for careers in fields such as manufacturing, construction, or software development.

senses.definition: a specialized institution of higher education that provides professional training and education in dentistry

examples.x: She spent four years in dental school to earn her Doctor of Dental Surgery degree.
The dental school curriculum includes courses in oral anatomy, dental procedures, and patient care.
After graduating from dental school, he opened his own dental practice in his hometown.
Dental schools often have clinics where students can gain practical experience under the supervision of licensed dentists.
Admission to dental school is highly competitive, with applicants needing to demonstrate academic excellence and strong interpersonal skills.

senses.definition: a college or university where students receive education and training to become pharmacists

examples.x: She attended pharmacy school to pursue her dream of becoming a pharmacist.
The pharmacy school offers courses in pharmacology, pharmaceutical sciences, and patient care.
He graduated from pharmacy school with a Doctor of Pharmacy degree.
Admission to the pharmacy school is competitive due to the high demand for pharmacists in healthcare settings.
The pharmacy school emphasizes hands-on training through internships and clinical rotations in various healthcare settings.

senses.definition: any educational institution formed to train individuals in visual or fine arts
an educational institution where students learn acting, stagecraft, and performance skills

examples.x: Drama schools typically offer courses in acting techniques, script analysis, and improvisation to prepare students for careers in theater and film.
Many aspiring actors attend drama school to refine their talents and gain experience in front of an audience.
Students at drama school often participate in workshops and rehearsals to develop their understanding of character development and storytelling.
Graduates from reputable drama schools may have opportunities to audition for major productions or join theater companies.
The curriculum at a drama school may also include classes in voice projection, movement, and theatrical history to provide a comprehensive education in the performing arts.

senses.definition: an educational institution where students learn about various aspects of filmmaking, including directing, producing, screenwriting, and cinematography

examples.x: He attended film school to pursue his passion for storytelling through cinema.
The renowned film school offers intensive programs in film production and screenwriting.
She honed her skills in cinematography at the prestigious film school in Los Angeles.
The film school's alumni have gone on to win awards at international film festivals.
He chose to enroll in a film school in New York City to immerse himself in the vibrant filmmaking community.

senses.definition: an institution that offers specialized training and education in various aspects of music, including performance, composition, theory, and music history

examples.x: She enrolled in a music school to study violin performance with a renowned instructor.
The music school at this university offers degree programs in music education, jazz studies, and music therapy.
After completing his studies at the music school, he became a professional pianist and composer.
Music schools often provide opportunities for students to participate in ensembles, concerts, and masterclasses.
Many aspiring musicians choose to attend music school to develop their skills and pursue careers in the music industry.

senses.definition: a college or university where students learn culinary skills, techniques, and knowledge related to cooking and food preparation

examples.x: She enrolled in a cooking school to pursue her passion for culinary arts and gastronomy.
The renowned cooking school offers professional chef training programs and recreational cooking classes.
He attended a cooking school in France to master classic French cooking techniques.
The cooking school's curriculum covers a wide range of cuisines and cooking styles from around the world.
She started her culinary career after graduating from a local cooking school specializing in pastry and baking.

senses.definition: an educational institution where students receive training in journalism, including reporting, writing, editing, and multimedia storytelling

examples.x: She enrolled in a journalism school to pursue her dream of becoming a journalist.
The prestigious journalism school offers degree programs in print, broadcast, and digital journalism.
He graduated from a journalism school with a strong foundation in investigative reporting and news production.
The journalism school provides hands-on experience through internships at local news organizations.
She chose to attend a journalism school in New York City to take advantage of its connections to the media industry.

senses.definition: an institution that primarily trains teachers for elementary education

examples.x: In the 19th century, normal schools played a crucial role in training teachers for rural communities.
She attended a normal school to earn her teaching certificate.
Normal schools focused on pedagogy, curriculum development, and classroom management.
After graduating from the normal school, he began his career as an elementary school teacher.
Normal schools evolved into modern teacher education programs offered by colleges and universities.

senses.definition: a school or college that people attend to for studying music, theater, or some other form of art

examples.x: The renowned conservatory offered intensive training programs in classical music, dance, and theater, attracting talented students from around the world.
She auditioned for admission to the prestigious conservatory, hoping to pursue her passion for acting and hone her skills under renowned instructors.
The conservatory's curriculum emphasized not only technical proficiency but also creativity and artistic expression, fostering a well-rounded education for aspiring performers.

Graduates of the conservatory went on to achieve success in their respective fields, landing leading roles on Broadway, performing with top orchestras, and exhibiting their artwork in galleries worldwide.
As a faculty member at the conservatory, he was dedicated to nurturing the next generation of artists and instilling in them a deep appreciation for their craft.

senses.definition: a group of eight private institutions of higher education in the United States known for their academic excellence, selective admissions policies, and competitive sports programs

examples.x: She was thrilled to be accepted into an Ivy League.
The Ivy League is known for its rigorous academic standards.
The Ivy League are renowned for their research contributions.
Attending an Ivy League can be a significant financial investment.
Her parents were proud that she received a scholarship to an Ivy League.

senses.definition: (in India) an institution recognized by the government as having university status, typically due to its academic excellence and capacity for research and teaching

examples.x: He pursued his Ph.D. at the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, known for its status as a prestigious deemed university.
She completed her undergraduate studies at the Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani, recognized as a renowned deemed university.
The Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai, offers various postgraduate programs as a deemed university.
He received specialized medical training at the All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi, a prominent deemed university.
She pursued a degree in fashion design at the National Institute of Fashion Technology, Delhi, which is recognized as a deemed university.

senses.definition: a type of secondary school that focuses on preparing students for higher education or university

examples.x: He attended a gymnasium where he studied a wide range of subjects, including mathematics, literature, and science.
Gymnasium students often have to pass rigorous entrance exams to gain admission.
After graduating from gymnasium, she plans to attend university to study medicine.
The curriculum at the gymnasium emphasizes critical thinking, problem-solving, and academic excellence.
Gymnasiums typically offer extracurricular activities such as sports, music, and debate clubs.

senses.definition: a department in a university or college that offers graduates an advanced or further degree

examples.x: After completing her undergraduate studies, she decided to apply to graduate school.
He was accepted into the graduate school of engineering at a prestigious university.
Graduate school programs typically focus on specialized fields of study.

senses.definition: an educational institution, often affiliated with a religious denomination, that provides training and education for individuals seeking to become clergy or religious leaders

examples.x: Seminaries often provide a combination of academic coursework, practical ministry experience, and spiritual formation.
Many seminaries require applicants to have a bachelor's degree and to undergo a discernment process before admission.

senses.definition: a Jewish educational institution where students study religious texts and teachings

examples.x: The rabbi spent many years studying at a yeshiva before becoming a spiritual leader.
At the yeshiva, students delve deep into the Talmud to gain a comprehensive understanding of Jewish law.
Sarah's son attends a prestigious yeshiva where he learns Hebrew, Torah, and Talmudic principles.
The yeshiva's curriculum emphasizes both religious studies and secular subjects to provide a well-rounded education.

senses.definition: a public school in England that has more freedom to control its curriculum and budget than traditional schools

examples.x: My daughter attends an academy school that specializes in performing arts education.
As a teacher at an academy school, I have more autonomy in designing my lesson plans and teaching methods.
The academy school's strong emphasis on sports has led to numerous championships and accolades for its student athletes.
We decided to enroll our son in an academy school known for its innovative approach to education and personalized learning experiences.

senses.definition: a type of secondary school in the UK that traditionally provided education in classical languages and literature, as well as mathematics and sciences

examples.x: During the Victorian era, grammar schools were prominent institutions for educating the middle and upper classes.
The government's education reforms aimed to increase access to grammar schools for students from all socioeconomic backgrounds.
In England, grammar schools typically admit students based on academic ability through selective entrance exams.
Some grammar schools have transitioned to become comprehensive schools, offering a broader curriculum to a more diverse student body.
The debate over grammar schools continues as policymakers discuss their role in modern education systems and strategies for improving social mobility.

senses.definition: a type of secondary school in the UK that provides a general education for students who do not attend grammar schools

examples.x: After taking the 11-plus exam, John was assigned to a secondary modern school rather than a grammar school.
Secondary modern schools focus on practical subjects and vocational training to prepare students for the workforce.
Mary attended a secondary modern school where she received a well-rounded education in various academic and technical subjects.
In the mid-20th century, secondary modern schools were established as part of the tripartite system of education in the UK.
The government's education reforms aimed to address inequalities by improving resources and opportunities in secondary modern schools.

senses.definition: a coaching center or school in Britain that offers intensive exam preparation courses, typically focusing on short-term study to achieve high scores

examples.x: The crammer down the street is known for its weekend crash courses for standardized tests.
Many students enroll in crammers during the summer holidays to get ready for college entrance exams.
The crammer in the city center offers specialized programs for various professional certification exams.
Parents often turn to crammers to help their children improve their grades before important school exams.
Some crammers provide personalized tutoring alongside group classes to cater to individual learning needs.

senses.definition: a type of state-funded secondary school in England that focuses on providing specialized education in technology and vocational subjects

examples.x: He attended a City Technology College known for its emphasis on science and engineering education.
The City Technology College offers advanced technology facilities and industry partnerships for hands-on learning.
She chose to enroll in a City Technology College to pursue her interest in computer programming and design.
The local City Technology College provides a comprehensive curriculum that includes vocational training in fields like electronics and mechanics.
He graduated from a City Technology College with qualifications in engineering and technology, preparing him for a career in the automotive industry.

senses.definition: a type of school in the UK that is run by the local government and often offers services beyond regular education, like clubs and healthcare

examples.x: The community school hosts a monthly food drive to support families in need.
I volunteered at the community school's literacy program to help children improve their reading skills.
Our local community school offers adult education classes in the evenings.
The community school's playground is open to the public on weekends.
The community school organized a fundraiser to build a new community garden for everyone to enjoy.

senses.definition: a type of school in England and Wales that is funded by the state but has more freedom to manage its own affairs, often with input from a governing body or foundation

examples.x: The local foundation school has its own board of governors responsible for managing the school's budget and policies.
Students at the foundation school benefit from a broad curriculum tailored to meet the needs and interests of the community.
The foundation school collaborates with local businesses and organizations to offer vocational training opportunities for its students.
She chose to enroll her child in the foundation school because of its strong emphasis on creative arts and technology.
The foundation school has a strong sense of community involvement, with parents and stakeholders actively participating in school decision-making processes.

senses.definition: a type of secondary school in England that provides technical and vocational education, often in partnership with local businesses and universities

examples.x: She enrolled in the university technical college to pursue her passion for computer science.
The university technical college partners with local industries to provide students with real-world experience.
His son attends the university technical college, where he studies advanced manufacturing and robotics.
The university technical college provides a pathway for students to pursue careers in STEM fields.

senses.definition: a type of independent school in England that is funded by the government but has more autonomy over its curriculum and management

examples.x: The local free school emphasizes outdoor education and experiential learning.
She chose to enroll her child in a free school because of its innovative teaching methods.
The free school's small class sizes allow for personalized attention and support.
He graduated from a free school specializing in performing arts and creative expression.
The free school provides a flexible schedule for students to pursue extracurricular interests alongside academics.

senses.definition: a school in England and Wales funded by the state but maintained by a religious or charitable organization, allowing for religious influence in admissions and education

examples.x: The local voluntary aided school integrates religious teachings into its curriculum.
Parents often choose voluntary aided schools for their strong emphasis on faith-based education.
The admissions process for a voluntary aided school may prioritize students from specific religious backgrounds.
Teachers at the voluntary aided school collaborate closely with religious leaders to uphold the school's ethos.
Voluntary aided schools receive funding from the government but are managed by religious or charitable organizations.

senses.definition: a type of school in England and Wales that is mainly funded and managed by the local authority but has a connection to a religious or charitable organization

examples.x: The local council supports the voluntary controlled school with funding and resources.
Children from different backgrounds attend the voluntary controlled school.
Parents appreciate the high-quality education provided by the voluntary controlled school.
The governing body of the voluntary controlled school includes representatives from the local community and religious foundation.
Teachers at the voluntary controlled school follow the national curriculum while incorporating the school's religious ethos.

senses.definition: a school in the UK for students aged 16 to 18, where they study advanced subjects before going to university or starting a career

examples.x: She attended a sixth-form college to study biology and chemistry at advanced levels.
The local sixth-form college offered a wide range of subjects, from humanities to sciences.
He chose to enroll in a sixth-form college to pursue his passion for art and design.
The sixth-form college provided excellent resources for students aiming to enter competitive fields like medicine or law.
After completing her GCSEs, she decided to attend a sixth-form college to explore career options in business and finance.

senses.definition: the university, college, or school that one used to study at

examples.x: She returned to her alma mater to give a guest lecture.
The alumni gathered to celebrate the centennial of their alma mater.
His alma mater awarded him an honorary degree for his achievements.
They visited their alma mater to tour the renovated library.
The alma mater's new scholarship program aims to support underprivileged students.

senses.definition: a prestigious British institution known for its traditional academic excellence and redbrick buildings

examples.x: The redbrick university in Manchester offers top-notch engineering programs.
Alumni from the redbrick universities often donate generously to support their alma maters.
Sarah plans to study law at a redbrick university in London next year.
The redbrick university's research on renewable energy has gained international recognition.
Admission to the redbrick universities is highly competitive due to their esteemed reputation.
